rows.what_you_get: A reviewable JSON policy that declares which tools your agent may call; everything else is refused by structure, not by a model guess.
gaps: Arg-value deny rules are a best-effort regex blocklist with no guarantee; keep irreversible tools off the allow-list rather than leaning on argument matching
State-dependent git laws (OFF_TRUNK, MERGE_HEAD) are decided by a stateful prefilter outside the manifest
rows.durability_note: Manifest is version-tagged; unknown fields/reasons/versions are a fatal load error (fail-loud). The loaded policy drives the real adjudication fold in tests, so the floor cannot silently go more-permissive.

rows.what_you_get: A one-shot oracle that tells you ALLOW/DENY for any tool call against a policy, with a structured refusal reason from a closed vocabulary — no model in the loop.
gaps: The 12-reason vocabulary is closed; adding a reason is a kernel change, not a config edit
No per-reason refusal-density metric is exposed by default
rows.durability_note: Default-deny on empty policy is load-bearing and witnessed; the fold is deterministic and tested at every adjudicator rank.

rows.what_you_get: Run an agent turn and watch a poisoned tool result get held out of the model's context — the injection never reaches the model, and the task still completes.
rows.witness: ctxmmu admit tests + testdata/poison.json; page-in gated on a witness Clear()
gaps: The detector is ~100% evadable by design and FP-prone on real corpora; the structural page-out (not the detector) is the moat
Inbound results are re-screened only at the persist boundary, not continuously mid-session
rows.durability_note: Quarantine survives the process boundary (recall) and a Clear() alone cannot launder poison — a fresh content re-screen is required on page-in.

rows.what_you_get: A deterministic, model-free screen in front of the context-MMU that strips unicode tricks and decodes base64/hex so an attacker cannot smuggle a secret past the regex floor by re-encoding it.
rows.witness: internal/normgate tests (6); cmd/ctxbench -chain measures 0->20/24 evasions caught
gaps: 4 of 24 evasions remain (pure-semantic paraphrase needs a classifier/IFC seam)
Enabled by one blank-import / FAK_WIRE_SCREEN, not a standalone surface a person runs on their own data
rows.durability_note: Deterministic, model-free, stdlib-only; demonstrated by cmd/ctxbench -chain on a real red-team battery. It activates inside the live agent/serve path, so its product surface is the agent, not itself.

rows.concept: Information-flow control + plan-CFI + effect-verifying witness gate
rows.what_you_get: The kernel stops believing the model: tainted data is sink-gated, a plan-CFI rung can require approval, and a 'ship' claim is refused unless corroborated by git evidence the agent did not author.
rows.witness: go test ./internal/ifc ./internal/plancfi ./internal/witness; TestDevAgentDefaultPath drives the real chain
gaps: The IFC ledger is per-trace; cross-trace taint flow is not modeled
Activating it at full power needs operator policy + witness wiring; there is no one-command product surface
The fine-tuned syscall/adjudication model that would consume the LabelRow corpus is not trained
rows.durability_note: Taint is stamped on the Ref at the call boundary (kernel-authored, not model-supplied); the witness gate fails closed when a claim is unwitnessed.

rows.concept: Local-model-on-the-wire screen (semantic + PII redaction + phash dedup)
rows.what_you_get: An additive, default-inert screen consulted after the regex floor that can quarantine a semantic injection, redact PII before bytes leave the box (with byte-exact restore), and collapse duplicate screenshots.
gaps: Default-inert (FAK_WIRE_SCREEN); the model-backed arm is build-tag gated and its latency is unmeasured, so it stays default-off
Byte removal reaches the wire only on the non-passthrough re-marshal path; the flagship Anthropic passthrough sends raw bytes until #555 lands
rows.durability_note: Deterministic heuristic floor is the shipped reference; the redactor pins the original in CAS so a wrong proposal costs one page-fault, never a lost fact.
